rationale: Kivetz, Urminsky, and Zheng (2006) is used repeatedly in the citing paper's theory and interpretation, not as a passing background mention. The citing paper relies on it to justify key mechanisms (goal-gradient effects and postreward resetting), and even uses it to motivate how reward proximity is operationalized. The authors explicitly position their contribution as taking this reward-pursuit logic from traditional loyalty programs into a gamified mobile-app context with hybrid rewards.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Kivetz et al. (2006) by transferring its goal-gradient/postreward-resetting framework from loyalty programs to mobile app gamification. It adds a hybrid reward architecture (game and value rewards), cross-engine interactions, and flow-based moderation of how engagement translates into value.
